extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon attacked with 4.5km remaining after CARAPAZ broke the race at 28.6km to go, winning the stage while CARAPAZ took pink from LÓPEZ Juan Pedro.
tactical_patterns: KELDERMAN Wilco paced downhill at 65km remaining, unusual positioning reveals Bora's willingness to burn climbers early on technical terrain.
CARAPAZ Richard's 28.6km solo attack forced pink change despite being caught, proving distant explosions work when group lacks unity.
NIBALI Vincenzo controlled final climb chase rather than attacking, veteran tactical restraint brought HINDLEY Jai into podium position economically.
YATES Simon attacked at 4.5km when 20 minutes down GC, exploiting group's reluctance to chase non-threats for stage win.

rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Took pink with 28.6km attack that opened 30 seconds, showing superior sustained power on hilly circuits.
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: Followed NIBALI's chase wheel to second place, moved to seven seconds behind pink through smart positioning.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Won stage from 20-minute deficit but knee injury plus Blockhaus weakness confirms GC ambitions dead.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: negative: Defended well initially but dropped to ninth, losing pink after holding it ten days reveals fading form.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: Fourth place and controlled final climb pace at 37 years old, rediscovering 2016 Giro-winning form surprisingly late.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Missed split at 65km but worked back on, finished third overall at 39 seconds shows strong recovery ability.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: Finished downhill pacing at 65km, deployed as road captain rather than protected GC rider by Bora.
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: Arrived in chase group at 28 seconds, veteran showing unexpected resilience on explosive hilly circuit.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Called out for missing split and continued losing time, 42-year-old unable to handle technical racing pace.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: Missed crucial 70km-to-go split, positioning weakness on short explosive stages remains clear liability.
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: negative: Tried repeatedly but ended out of picture, lacks acceleration to survive when Bora detonates race early.
